review.summary: The code has migrated to Swiss Style successfully but contains several critical issues: hardcoded SCORE_CATEGORIES don't match the dynamic lens structure, potential API key exposure, and missing error handling for API failures. The system prompt architecture is well-designed but the scoring display logic assumes a fixed schema.

issues.description: Hardcoded SCORE_CATEGORIES (company_stage_fit, role_type, mission_alignment) don't match the actual lens v2.15 structure which uses different scoring dimensions. The UI will fail to display scores for dimensions not in this hardcoded list.
issues.fix: Either dynamically generate score categories from result.scores keys, or update SCORE_CATEGORIES to match the actual lens dimensions from the profile context
issues.code_snippet: Object.keys(result.scores || {}).map(key => ({ key, score: result.scores[key], label: key.replace(/_/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, l => l.toUpperCase()) }))

issues.description: No API key is included in the Anthropic API request headers. This will result in 401 authentication errors.
issues.fix: Add API key to headers or use environment variable

issues.description: HTTP error responses from the Anthropic API are not checked. A 4xx/5xx response will be processed as if successful, leading to confusing error messages.
issues.fix: Check response.ok and handle API errors appropriately
issues.code_snippet: if (!response.ok) { const errorData = await response.json(); throw new Error(`API Error ${response.status}: ${errorData.error?.message || 'Request failed'}`); }

issues.description: JSON.parse() can throw but the catch block only logs err.message || 'Scoring failed'. If the API returns malformed JSON, users get a generic error instead of useful debugging info.
issues.fix: Provide more specific error messages for JSON parsing failures
issues.code_snippet: } catch (parseError) { throw new Error(`Failed to parse scoring response: ${parseError.message}`); }

issues.description: These functions handle classification values but don't have fallbacks for unexpected classification names from the lens document.
issues.fix: Add default cases or validation for classification values
issues.code_snippet: const classColor = (c) => { const colors = { STRONG_FIT: '#1A1A1A', GOOD_FIT: '#E8590C', MARGINAL: '#666', SKIP: '#D93025' }; return colors[c] || '#999'; }

issues.description: The lens length validation (> 500 chars) is arbitrary and could exclude valid but concise lens documents.
issues.fix: Consider more robust validation like checking for expected YAML frontmatter or key sections

missed_edge_cases: What if the lens document has different scoring dimension names than expected?
What if result.scores is null or has unexpected structure?
What if the API returns a different model response format?
What if the lens document is valid but uses different classification thresholds?
positive_observations: Excellent Swiss Style migration - proper colors, typography, and layout
Well-structured 3-tier system prompt architecture separating universal instructions from lens-specific and output formatting
Good lens status management with fallback options for manual paste
Comprehensive result display covering all expected output fields from the scoring system
Clean separation of concerns between data fetching, processing, and rendering
known_bug_check: Flat bonuses override slider weights: NOT AFFECTED - this code only displays results, doesn't implement the scoring logic where bonuses dominate sliders
Beam false-negative (March 2026): NOT AFFECTED - this code uses the dynamically fetched lens v2.15 which has the corrected gate parameters
Discrepancy between scorer JSX gates and n8n pipeline: NOT AFFECTED - this code uses the lens document as the source of truth rather than hardcoded gates
